source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A 3-pyramid is a stack of the following 3 layers of balls. In the same way we have a 4-pyramid, a 5-pyramid, etc. All the outside balls of an 8-pyramid are removed. What kind of figure form the rest balls?
<image1>
extraction_notes: answer field is an option letter and was mapped to the corresponding option text

image_dependency: question text refers to a pictured '3-pyramid' made of layers of balls
image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: must infer the structure/pattern of an n-pyramid from the illustrated example
must reason about removing all outside balls from an 8-pyramid to identify the remaining inner figure
multiple-choice answer requires mapping the derived remaining figure to one of 5 named pyramid sizes
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a discrete target answer ('4-pyramid')
choices provide a closed set of 5 possible outputs
result can be checked by geometric/combinatorial reasoning about layered ball pyramids

risk_flags: high image dependence for understanding the depicted pyramid structure
surface-interpretation ambiguity: 'outside balls' may need precise definition

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, with registry_passed=true and no registry issues
high verifiability score (0.94) with a discrete short-text target and closed-set choices supports reliable checking
image dependence is high (0.89), but the required image asset is available, so core semantics appear recoverable
multi-step reasoning value is substantial (0.74), making it a useful candidate despite some interpretation ambiguity
the noted ambiguity around 'outside balls' is a manageable risk rather than a loss of core semantic support
